title: Demonstrate vptr Size Impact
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the program to print whether a virtual class is larger than a plain class, demonstrating the vptr overhead.

## Input
No input.

## Output
Print exactly one line:
```
vptr adds overhead: yes
```

## Requirements
1. Define `Plain` with one `int` member and no virtual functions.
2. Define `WithVirtual` with one `int` member and one virtual function `void f()`.
3. Compare `sizeof(WithVirtual) > sizeof(Plain)`.
4. Print `vptr adds overhead: yes` if `WithVirtual` is larger, `vptr adds overhead: no` otherwise.
5. On any standard 64-bit implementation `WithVirtual` will be larger — your solution code must produce `yes`.

title: Inherited vtable Slot Demo
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the program to demonstrate that a derived class inherits vtable slots it does not override.

## Input
No input.

## Output
Print exactly three lines:
```
Animal::move
Dog::speak
Animal::move
```

## Requirements
1. `Animal` has `virtual void speak() const` printing `Animal::speak` and `virtual void move() const` printing `Animal::move`.
2. `Dog` overrides only `speak()` to print `Dog::speak`; it does not override `move()`.
3. `main` creates an `Animal a` and a `Dog d`.
4. Store both in `Animal* ptrs[2] = {&a, &d}`.
5. Print `ptrs[0]->move()` then `ptrs[1]->speak()` then `ptrs[1]->move()`.
6. The output shows `Animal::move` for both `move()` calls, proving the inherited slot.
